ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-18-2024 Continued To Oct-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Rivers Williams, Sr. and the Attorney Anne E. Steele to appear on October 16, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to October 16, 2024.

An order to show cause re sanctions is also being issued to Attorney Anne E. Steele for the same date for counsel to appear and show cause as to why sanctions under CCP 128 in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-09-2023 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anne Steele appeared by zoom for and with Rivers Williams, Sr. by zoom.
Angelic Williams appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court stated that it is not inclined to delay appointment any longer, that there are a lot of procedural issues with Kamailia and Angelic's petition, and that it thinks Rivers' petition is in order. Discussion was held as to whether or not an evidentiary hearing is needed, the reasons why the parties feel that the appointment of the opposing party would not be appropriate, the issue of having an independent fiduciary appointed, family dynamics, whether it is appropriate for the court to order a bond when the will waives bond, whether or not there are any assets in the estate, etc. The court overrules the objections filed by Kamailia and Angelic. The court will grant the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary filed by Rivers Williams. The lost will dated June 10, 2016 is admitted to probate. Rivers Williams is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. The court intends to order a bond in the amount of $832,000.00, but will take this issue under submission. Status on close of estate is set for September 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar. Shirley Yawitz is appointed probate referee. Kamailia and Angelic's petition is denied.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Continued To Aug-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Continued To Aug-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anne Steele, Esq., counsel for Rivers Williams, Sr., Petitioner; Angelica Williams and Kamailia Williams, Petitioners in pro per (all appearances by video conference).

The Court notes the filing of a competing petition by Angelica Williams and Kamailia Williams, jointly, but that it has not yet had time to review the same. Ms. Steele reports the same and that she tried to contact Mr. Madison, counsel for Angelica Williams and Kamailia Williams, who advised that he no longer represents them. Angelica and Kamailia Williams confirm that they are now in pro per.

Court and counsel discuss whether objection or opposition to the Williams' petition would be filed and if so, when. After discussion, Ms. Steele will file any opposition on or before July 26, 2023. All matters, including the Williams' petition, are continued to August 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Singh. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-03-2023 Continued To Jun-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: Anne Steele appeared by zoom for Rivers Williams, Sr. (petitioner).
Jonathan Maddison appeared by zoom for and with Angelic Williams by zoom and for Kamailia Williams (objectors).
Ejackie Ford appeared by zoom in proria persona.

The court finds that the notice and publication requirement is functionally satisfied. Mrs. Steele stated that there is no money in the estate whatsoever, that there is a designated house in the trust of the decedent's mother and that the estimated value of the real property is about $800,000.00. Discussion was held regarding the $32,000.00 in rental income that was received from the house after the decedent's death which was used to pay the taxes and the insurance, the parties not knowing how much debt the trust has, the search that was done by Mrs. Steele's client to locate the original will, the decedent's will, Mr. Rivers will and trust documents having gone missing, the allegation of said documents having been stolen, the allegation of someone ordering a hitman to harm Mr. Williams, Mr. Maddison's clients' having no knowledge of a will or a trust for the decedent, and believing that there is a family trust, there being no competing petition, there being no explanation as to why the Ford Trust residence wasn't distributed before Ella died, Mrs. Steele not knowing status of the property, but believing that there are tenants, the family dynamics, Ejackie's position with regard to accusations that were made against her and wanting to clear her name, etc. The court stated that it needs testimony about the search that was done to locate the original will. The court orders counsel to meet and confer and figure out a path forward. The Second Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to June 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-08-2023 Continued To May-03-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anne Steele, via Zoom, for Rivers Williams, Sr.; Jonathan Madison, via Zoom, for and with Angelic Williams and Kamailia Williams, via Zoom, Objectors.

At the court's query, Mr. Madison advises that he intends to file an objection to the amended petition. The court advises Ms. Steele that she needs to clear all procedural issues as advised to her via Examiner's notes. The court and counsel discuss their respective client's positions and advises that it intends to continue the matter to allow for the filing of an opposition to the amended petition and to remedy the procedural issues. Upon query by Ms. Steele, the court confirms that the continued date will not be for an evidentiary hearing, but the court will decide at that time how to move this matter forward depending on the position of the case.

The matter is continued to May-03-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Mr. Madison is instructed to file his opposition to the amended petition by Apr-19-2023. Any response to the preceding is to be filed by Apr-26-2023.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
